_comment.1: Open-loop plant Bode of the x/tilt axis: command -> FSM -> camera -> CoM.
_comment.2: SOFTWARE injection: bode_plot replaces the x element (1; CoM is [y, x])
_comment.3: with a sine each iteration, and piplate_bridge writes offset + sine*scale
_comment.4: to DAC ch1 every cycle. Camera/CoM settings match the current steering
_comment.5: geometry (32x32, exp 250, gain 100). Whole-frame CoM, no
_comment.6: tracking/acquisition: bode_plot holds the command at 0 (fine channel
_comment.7: parked at bias) for start_delay 30 s so the coarse channels can adjust,
_comment.7b: then sweeps; there must be no acquisition phase after that point.
_comment.8: No PID is in the loop, so the command IS the sine = open loop. Phase is
_comment.9: measured from wall-clock time and includes the full transport delay
_comment.10: (measured separately: ~1.7 ms at this frame rate).
_comment.11: baud 921600: the port is USB CDC so the wire ignores baud, but the
_comment.12: bridge paces itself by it -- at 115200 it would cap at ~440 writes/s
_comment.13: and mangle the injected sine at a 2310 Hz loop rate.
_comment.14: y/tip (ch0) and the coarse channels are parked at their bias voltages.

params._roi_note: ROI centred on beam at coarse bias 2/2 (fine 1.5/2.15): probe-verified 2026-07-21, full-sensor CoM (x 919, y 507). Re-derive via contrib/probe_frame.json only on an alignment change.

pipeline._comment: frame -> UDP viewer, so you can watch the beam + tracking (same as steering_fsp)

params._note: tracked 15x15 window, threshold 30 -- matches attenuation_test.json exactly so the sweep measures the same sensor the loop sees. Initialized on the beam (frame centre) with no acquisition phase: bode_plot injects from the first frame, and the stray reflection at ROI (10,28) must stay out of the sum. Output is image-normalized.

pipeline._comment: CoM -> UDP viewer so the tracked centroid can be overlaid on the frame
Replace the x/tilt element (1) with the swept sine; read CoM x as response

params._tuning: v2 (2026-07-17): 60 log points x 49 cycles, split into 7 integer-cycle segments per point. The segment scatter gives the per-point error bar + a coherence quality (1=clean; <0.9 flagged as a disturbance line or drift inside the window). Integer-cycle segment boundaries (on excitation-phase crossings) kill the partial-cycle DC/line leakage the old fixed-time cutoff left in. 49 cycles keeps the 5 Hz window ~10 s, resolving it from the 5.64 Hz line.
params._amplitude2: second full sweep at half amplitude: if |H| moves between the two, the DAC is clipping or the CoM has left its linear region and K is not trustworthy. bode_plot logs the worst-case change and flags >10%.
params._avoid_freqs: nudge sweep points off the measured bench lines (a line inside the lock-in skirt biases the point coherently -- segments do not average it out). List from attenuation6/8 memory.
params.config: x/tilt open-loop plant, coarse bias 2.0/2.0 (beam realigned, matches steering_tuned.json operating point), 32x32 exp250 gain100 bw100, beam-centred ROI via start_x/start_y, tracked 20x20 thr22. Re-bode at the new bias to refresh Kx/tau for the loop retune. K uses <=30 Hz; tau (delay) uses the full 5-300 Hz phase slope, so the sweep must run to 300.
params._start_delay: hold injection at 0 (= fine channel parked at its bias) for 30 s so the coarse channels have time to adjust before the sweep starts

pipeline._note: same mapping as steering_tuned.json: ch0=y(idx0, parked), ch1=x(idx1, driven), ch2/ch3 coarse parked. skip_unchanged: only the driven channel costs wire time. Coarse offsets 2.0/2.0 = current operating point (beam realigned, matches steering_tuned.json) so the sweep measures the plant the tuned loop actually sees; earlier: 1.5/2.15 (07-21), 1.2/2.45 (07-20), 1.98/1.97 (07-17), 1.95/2.02 (07-16), 2.52/1.62 (07-14), 2.7/1.65, 2.6/1.4 (07-13), 1.05/2.35, 0.8/2.394 (07-10).
